title: hibbertia pustulata
intro: Hibbertia pustulata, also known as the Orange Jewelled Guinea Flower, is a flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Dilleniaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Hibbertia pustulata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Australian medicine.
